Title: Metabolic engineering of terpene biosynthesis in plants using a trichome-specific transcription factor from spearmint

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: secondary metabolites used as flavours, fragrances and pharmaceuticals
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher MsNTT transcript abundance is associated with increased monoterpene-producing MsYABBY5 RNAi lines, whereas lower expression is associated with reduced-monoterpene MsYABBY5 overexpression lines; suggested to support metabolic activity by ATP import into plastids
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MsNTT encodes a plastidic ATP/ADP transporter-like protein and localizes to the plastid membrane. 2. MsNTT transcript levels increase in MsYABBY5 RNAi lines and decrease in MsYABBY5 overexpression lines. 3. The paper suggests enhanced ATP import into plastids may contribute to higher metabolic activity and terpene production, but MsYABBY5 does not directly bind the MsNTT promoter.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; subcellular localization by fluorescent protein fusion; EMSA negative result for direct MsYABBY5 binding
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected in spearmint PGT transcriptome; differential expression in MsYABBY5 transgenic lines
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Differential in MsYABBY5 RNAi and overexpression leaves
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-Seq of spearmint PGT (accession not provided)
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential supporting target to enhance plastid energy supply for specialized metabolism, but causal role in terpene increase remains indirect in this paper
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors conclude MsNTT is probably not a direct transcriptional target of MsYABBY5 and may reflect indirect flux-related regulation.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: YABBY family; N-terminal C2C2 zinc finger domain; C-terminal YABBY helix-loop-helix-like domain
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: monoterpenes (limonene, carvone); additionally affects diterpenes, monoterpenes, sesquiterpenes and eugenol in heterologous systems
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: secondary metabolites used as flavours, fragrances and pharmaceuticals
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: peltate glandular trichomes (PGT) of aerial tissues
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a negative regulator of secondary metabolism; silencing increases spearmint monoterpene production, whereas overexpression decreases terpene accumulation in spearmint, Nicotiana sylvestris and sweet basil and also reduces eugenol in basil
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MsYABBY5 is preferentially expressed in spearmint peltate glandular trichomes. 2. RNAi knockdown increased total monoterpene production in spearmint by 20%–77%, while 35S overexpression reduced total monoterpenes by 23%–52%. 3. Ectopic expression reduced CBT-diol in Nicotiana sylvestris and reduced monoterpenes, sesquiterpenes and eugenol in sweet basil. 4. MsYABBY5 binds the MsWRKY75 promoter and activates its expression in transient assays, supporting a regulatory network that represses terpene production.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi silencing; overexpression; GC-MS metabolite profiling; qRT-PCR; in situ hybridization; transient transactivation assay; EMSA; subcellular localization by YFP fusion and BFA treatment
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent negative regulator/repressor of glandular trichome secondary metabolism with strong causal effects on terpene output
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation of MsWRKY75; overall indirect repression of terpene and broader secondary metabolite accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates MsWRKY75 promoter; does not significantly alter transcript levels of tested monoterpene biosynthetic genes or MEP-pathway genes; MsNTT expression changes appear indirect
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: secondary metabolism flux into terpene biosynthesis and broader glandular-trichome specialized metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Promoter contains light-responsive, MeJA-responsive and gibberellin-responsive cis-elements; functional upstream signals not directly tested
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus; cytoplasm; Golgi-associated/secretory pathway-related localization observed before BFA treatment
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Preferentially/highly expressed in peltate glandular trichomes of young spearmint leaves; not detected in surrounding leaf tissue by in situ hybridization
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: BFA treatment shifted localization to nucleus; promoter analysis suggests possible light, methyl jasmonate and gibberellin responsiveness
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-Seq of spearmint leaves/PGT (accession not provided); GC-MS metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter for overexpression and RNAi in spearmint; native MsYABBY5 promoter is trichome-specific in Nicotiana benthamiana and drives PGT/head-cell expression in basil/tobacco
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Downregulation or editing of MsYABBY5 is a promising strategy to increase terpene yield in glandular trichomes; trichome-specific MsYABBY5 promoter may be useful for targeted metabolic engineering
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Ectopic overexpression in sweet basil caused curled leaves and delayed flowering; no obvious phenotype reported for spearmint RNAi lines
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MsYABBY5 is unusual among tested MsYABBY proteins in showing dual nuclear/cytoplasmic localization and Golgi association. The increase in metabolites upon silencing was not explained by transcriptional induction of known monoterpene pathway genes, suggesting upstream flux control.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: secondary metabolites used as flavours, fragrances and pharmaceuticals
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Proposed negative regulator of terpene production downstream of MsYABBY5
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MsWRKY75 is enriched in spearmint peltate glandular trichomes. 2. Its transcript level decreases in MsYABBY5 RNAi lines. 3. MsYABBY5 binds a promoter region of MsWRKY75 and activates MsWRKY75 promoter activity in transient assays. 4. The paper proposes that MsWRKY75 probably represses terpene production, but direct functional validation of MsWRKY75 on metabolites was not performed.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-Seq expression profiling; qRT-PCR; EMSA for promoter binding by MsYABBY5; transient promoter activation assay in Nicotiana benthamiana
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Candidate downstream repressor proposed in the MsYABBY5 regulatory network; not directly established by gain/loss-of-function metabolite assays
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: putative transcriptional repression of terpene biosynthesis genes/pathway
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-Seq of spearmint PGT (accession not provided)
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential downstream target for future engineering of terpene production, but requires direct validation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core regulatory gene because it is experimentally followed in the Results as a direct downstream target of MsYABBY5, although its metabolite effect remains inferential rather than directly demonstrated.
